purpose: Evaluate whether priority jurisdictions have policy records covering the core governance functions required for CCUS deployment. Coverage status is curated from official-source review; SQLite policy IDs provide the evidence anchor.

status_definitions.covered: At least one current policy record directly and materially covers the governance function.
status_definitions.partial: Policy support exists, but the function is incomplete, indirect, planned, or dependent on broader legislation.
status_definitions.missing: No current policy record directly covers the governance function. This is a research gap, not evidence that no policy exists.
status_definitions.not_applicable: The function is not assessed separately for this jurisdiction.

dimensions.label: Legal and regulatory basis
dimensions.definition: Primary legislation or equivalent rules that authorize and govern capture, transport, utilization or geological storage.

dimensions.label: Incentives and finance
dimensions.definition: Tax credits, contracts, grants, regulated revenue or financing programmes that materially improve project bankability.

dimensions.label: Storage and permitting
dimensions.definition: Site selection, pore-space access, injection permits, monitoring, closure, financial assurance and long-term liability.

dimensions.label: CO2 transport and market access
dimensions.definition: Pipeline, shipping, network access, tariff, interoperability or shared-infrastructure market arrangements.

dimensions.label: MRV and accounting
dimensions.definition: Measurement, reporting, verification, emissions accounting, crediting or compliance rules that recognize captured and stored CO2.

legal_regulatory.rationale: The federal Class VI UIC framework and delegated state primacy provide the core injection-well regulatory basis.

incentives_finance.rationale: Section 45Q provides the principal federal operating incentive, with the 2025 amendments changing parity and entity restrictions.

storage_permitting.rationale: Class VI governs permitting, construction, testing, monitoring, financial responsibility, closure and post-injection site care.

transport_market.rationale: CIFIA supplies dedicated federal finance for shared common-carrier CO2 transport infrastructure, while broader tariff, access and market-governance rules are not represented as a single national framework.

mrv_accounting.rationale: Class VI monitoring and federal greenhouse-gas reporting interact with 45Q substantiation requirements.

legal_regulatory.rationale: The Energy Act 2023 establishes the economic licensing and regulatory architecture for CO2 transport and storage.

incentives_finance.rationale: The Industrial Carbon Capture business model provides selected projects with long-term contractual revenue support and capital co-funding.

storage_permitting.rationale: Existing legislation and licensing arrangements provide the core storage and transport regulatory basis.

transport_market.rationale: The market vision proposes a network code, but the code remains a planned governance component rather than a final rule.

mrv_accounting.rationale: The legal and contractual frameworks require metering and verified storage, while a dedicated cross-chain accounting record is not separately captured.

legal_regulatory.rationale: The 2024 CCUS Act and its enforcement decree establish and operationalize the national framework for capture, transport, storage and utilization.

incentives_finance.rationale: The decree details enterprise and project-support procedures, but no dedicated verified regulated-revenue or universal CCUS incentive is currently recorded.

storage_permitting.rationale: The Act and decree provide the framework and detailed procedures for storage exploration, business approvals, safety management and monitoring.

transport_market.rationale: Transport approvals, pipeline safety and inspections are operationalized, but dedicated network-access and tariff rules are not separately recorded.

mrv_accounting.rationale: The decree operationalizes storage monitoring, safety inspection, evaluation and reporting requirements under the framework Act.

legal_regulatory.rationale: The EU CCS Directive and its Dutch Mining Act implementation provide the supranational and national legal basis for geological storage.

incentives_finance.rationale: SDE++ is the principal Dutch operating-support mechanism for industrial decarbonization, including CCS.

storage_permitting.rationale: EU storage requirements are implemented through the Dutch Mining Act exploration, storage-permit and supervision framework.

transport_market.rationale: No dedicated Dutch CO2 transport, network-access or tariff policy record is currently mapped.

mrv_accounting.rationale: EU monitoring and accounting requirements apply through the Dutch storage-permitting and supervision framework.

legal_regulatory.rationale: National standards and sectoral carbon-market rules provide components, but China does not yet have a dedicated national CCS permitting and liability law.

incentives_finance.rationale: Central-bank and demonstration support can finance eligible low-carbon projects, but a dedicated CCUS revenue mechanism is not established.

storage_permitting.rationale: No dedicated national policy record currently governs pore-space rights, injection licensing, closure and long-term storage liability.

transport_market.rationale: No dedicated national CO2 pipeline, shipping, third-party access or tariff framework is currently recorded.

mrv_accounting.rationale: Standards and ETS MRV capacity are developing, but a complete cross-chain CCUS accounting framework is not yet recorded.
